reviews.review_text:
THIS GAME NEEDS MORE CONTENT. DON T LET NIGHTEREIGN DIE. 

I am leaving this negative review because FromSoftware and Bandai Namco have completely abandoned Elden Ring Nightreign.

This game has a brilliant cooperative and roguelite foundation, but it severely lacks variety. We are feeling completely left behind. We supported this project from day one, and instead of giving it the long-term content and updates it deserves, the developers dropped a couple of minor patches and walked away to focus on other franchises.

Just because FromSoftware has achieved massive success with Dark Souls and the main Elden Ring game doesn't mean they can treat players of their spin-offs as secondary customers. This absolute lack of post-launch support heavily damages the studio's prestige and reputation.

We DO NOT WANT this game to die. We demand substantial content updates, more boss variety, classes, or whatever. Until FromSoftware shows they care about this community and adds real content, this review will remain negative.

(To everyone reading this: if you feel as abandoned as we do, do not stay silent. Leave your own negative review, spread the word, and vote this up. The only way to pressure FromSoftware and Bandai Namco into listening is by hitting their score. Join the protest and make our voices heard <3)
Fail. Struggle. Fear. Rise. Overcome. Never stop. Even the longest night ends. Dawn is near. Believe.

What a beautiful game. I never expected that I would like ELDEN RING + Rouge like this much and accidentally spend 200 hours in it within 2 months, but it was well worth it. 
10 Nightlords, 10 Nightfarers, and 5 Shifting Earths seem like enough content, but something in my heart still expects more maybe SOTE weapons, Ed Holster, and Dreglord atleast.
FROMSOFTWARE DO SOMETHING....
Great long-term investment game! Awesome to play with friends (and rage about when you inevitably fail)

A very exciting game, especially if you have 2 people with skill issues accompanying you, it will be 300% more fun than solo and random anomalies, special don ranked, which adds variety and my playtime becomes 800h+, Pls Fromsoft update this peak game with more content because this game has a lot of potential Than expected and don't forget raven- uh i mean recluse summer suit skin.

Great game, but talk about wasted potential!

There are so many characters to level up, each with missions of their own, not to mention every boss has multiple difficulty levels on top of their enhanced versions.

Also, they FIXED bows in Souls-likes as a whole... wow, they're great.

Depths of Night is the cherry on top. Don't listen to Redditors calling it "Elden Ring Fortnite"—that's probably the most insane criticism possible and screams, "I've never played it." Yes, it has duos. Yes, you can play solo... but why would you want to?

I hope FromSoftware learns from the mistake of abandoning this game. I genuinely hope and wish for more updates.

On top of all that, it runs amazingly on Steam Deck.

Wonderful spin-off with a fun gameplay loop. I wish my friends played with me, but I ended up making some new ones along the way.

If you haven't purchased this yet, trust me you get your money's worth.

Ironeye mains RISE UP!
A good game with random matchmaking or solo, but a fantastic game with one or two friends. Even if they throw runs. A lot. You know who you are.

Good game, terrible community. The devs actively punish the player who is stuck in a match where their teammates DC because YOU will get a DC penalty if you leave a match despite being the ONLY person in it. If you get the game, just find a group of friends or a Discord group and then play the game together or just mod it so you can play offline without having to deal with matchmaking and the many, many lost matches due to players who leave, grief, cheat, or more.
A fun idea and executed rather well, with amazing bosses and replayability, however some new weapons and more dlc would go a loooong way in keeping the game fun and relevent.

This is the best ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game I have ever played I ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ LOVE THIS GAME. I think they made a masterpiece with this one. I am citing my best friend Cahal McCreesh as a reference for this review as he has also said it is his favourite game ever made. I have secured PABLO!!

MITCHELL

We DO NOT WANT this game to die. We demand substantial content updates, more boss variety, classes, or whatever. Until FromSoftware shows they care about this community and adds real content, this review will remain negative.
started playing the game again after 6 months and it constantly crashes

The game's anniversary just passed, and players who have been begging the developers for new content for six months now were left with nothing. Until the devs start properly supporting this amazing game, the review will stay negative (which means [b] forever [/b] probably).

At first I was frustrated how fast-paced and difficult the game was for a beginner. But once you play you realize the game's art, systems, animations are so complex that few other games can match. The relic/scene system (essentially a dice roll for perks) is a nice touch which allows me to experiment with different builds for different characters. I love the Physical Attack Up +2 relic for Wylder. Last thing I want to add is this game seems to be purpose-built around multiplayer co-op with your teammates' builds shining in different areas. Solo singleplayer deprives you of that aspect of the game.

I cannot accurately explain if this game is good or not. I've had some of my highest highs and lowest lows thanks to this cesspool of souls variety friendslop and my opinion is skewed thanks to my years of souls experience. 10/10 would recommend that you get gud.

This game is really fun if you like roguelikes, soulslikes, terminal illness and depression! I love playing this game when I feel good about myself and need a humbling experience. If you have any self-love or dignity, this game is for you because you don't deserve it. 

Edit : NEVER PLAY THIS GAME!!! Unless you enjoy torture and are also looking for a reason to commit, DONT PLAY!

Lotta a fun for a while, a must if you are a fan from the series and even better if you friends to play with, if you don't ... oh boy let me tell you something... STAY AWAY FROM DEEP OF THE NIGHT MODE, just trust me bro!

This game is insanely fun. Dont expect Elden Ring or another Soulslike. Not a lot of time to pause and take in scenery. Its very fast paced. But its great co-op. The cinematics and artwork are fantastic as always. And once you get the hang of it its not as stressful as other Soulslikes. I'm hooked.

Generally not even lying, this game easily has the coolest boss-fights of all fromsoft games, its visually impressive  and sound tracks are a blessing for the ears, the night lords are just crazy good made and if you allready enjoy roguelikes this might be the best game ever for you, when the game came out first it had some issues but those got fixed over time, due to the genre of this game it also has no archievemnts that can be missed differnt than the ordinary fromsoft games like dark souls or elden ring, in generally beautifull game, i recommend.
Too little content, should've been a live service from the get-go, the game is insanely fun and all, but after you beat all the bosses, it becomes repetitive and lackluster

If you have friends, the game is very fun
If you don't have friend, you should log off and reflect on your life brother

"Ok, Miyazaki. You're gonna make a game that's based entirely around being online. Then, you're gonna give it no communication features beyond a basic ping system. THEN you're gonna not do crossplay!"

One of the best games to play with friends. This was by far my favorite purchase I've made with friends. The character builds, bosses, and random events are the highlight of this game. Nightreign has easily over 50 hours of content in it; making it one of the longest and best experiences a co-op game can offer. The solo experience, however, is my only negative. It's clearly designed for multiple friends or matchmaking.

There's a big tiddy baddy
There's a katana guy 
And
There are multiple characters with ultis that create absolute cinema moments
If you're a weeb you'll like this game

Although I have a little over 11 hours on Steam, I have over 250+ on console. I've beaten all bosses, both and ED and normal, and have all completed all remembrance questlines. I mention this because although I've successfully unlocked all possible content, the game is still an absolute blast. Nightreign was the first soulslike game where I felt like every playstyle is fully viable, especially when the different aspects of the game are heavily based around teamwork and combining abilities/skills. Each boss feels fun to face (outside of Augur, that fight is a snoozefest and I honestly believe it was intentionally made as an "easy boss" for farming remembrance quests), and most of them feel rewarding as you're granted with a relic if you beat that specific nightlord. Most of them are fun and help with making interesting builds, while others definitely fall short in consideration of the boss and it's difficulty. The pros about Nightreign can go on and on, but I'll list some cons as a hopeful warning before anyone picks it up, or maybe if From sees this and for some reason uses it as a suggestion. Firstly, this game has received no content in nearly the last 7 months as of writing this, with the last balance patch being in January of 2026. From has announced their intentions with the game previously and have stated it was simply a test game for their future multiplayer ideas, and they have been fully focused on other projects. This likely means Nightreign may never seen new content, which leads me into the next point; replayability. I believe this is a game where you get what you paid for, and that the game will eventually begin to feel stale after some time. What doesn't help is the lack of new balance patches, the minority of players that disconnect after their first down, and the rather extreme cheating issues on pc servers. I believe the game would heavily benefit from crossplay, but the cheating and usage of cheated in relics with unrealistic stats make the game significantly harder to enjoy, ESPECIALLY in the Deep of Night mode ("ranked" system, runs get more challenging as points are rewarded for victories). With that being said, I do think the game is blast in solos if that's your preference, but the nightlords were designed with 3-player co-op in mind and can feel a bit weak under certain conditions or on certain characters. If you really want to get this game, I would absolutely recommend it as there isn't anything like it that I have found. The game is overall cheaper than most newer releases and the dlc being only another $15 USD is pretty nice addition. You can even add more to your experience by modding in new content yourself, just make sure to only use it in offline mode as to not break any rules. That being said, please be ready to purchase a game that may never receive any future content, and that you'll only get as much value from it as you see fit. If Fromsoft ever reads this, please continue to add periodical updates to this game. Even if it's just balance patches and previous assets and bosses from other games, it'll easily spark some more love back into this game and it's community. It's definitely a game that you could keep going for years. I hope this helps anyone curious about getting the game!

Leaving a negative review because the way this game was treated by fromsoft is truly appalling. Incredible gameplay that was completely abandoned with too little content, turning it into a complete failure of a roguelike with extremely minimal content, lack of variety, and very limited replayability, which goes completely against what a roguelike game should be like. 

Wasted potential is the only thing that comes to mind when I think of this game. ♥♥♥♥ you, fromsoft.
FROMSOFTTTTT!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! GIVE ME A CONTENT UPDATE OR SECOND DLC FOR NIGHTREIGN (or bring duskbloods to pc after it comes out)!!!!!!!!!! AND MY LIFE IS YOURS

I absolutely cannot play the random co-op mode in Deep of Night because I constantly encounter teammates who leave the match whenever they want. If I want to play alone, Elden Ring is a better option than Nightreign. Therefore, I hope there will be harsher penalties for players who ruin matches like that.
I love this game but its lacking of new contents. But FromSoftware please update new content

Absolutely horrible game. No creativity, no good mechanics, no good characters terrible map design. Just absolutely horrendous. I mean, lets be honest. How do you drop all these good games and then drop the most ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ heaping pile of game called "Elden ring Nightreign". Here's what I genuinely hate about this game, the game. Now, I loved Eden ring. But a multiplayer Elden ring? terrible.

 10/10
would recommend
captured lighting in a bottle, Combined interesting aspects and melded them together some what perfectly BUT. 1 year of absolute nothing they just let the game die.

Nightreign came out at a time where I feel like I didn't properly appreciate it. I played it on release, and enjoyed it decently enough, but bounced pretty quick. Couldn't even tell you why. Maybe I was just too busy playing more ER DLC and other games. But diving back into it now is eye opening. The combat is fast, almost like Bloodborne but without making it mindless hyperviolence. The characters each have their own specific niches that all feel really satisfying to play, and they even have unique movesets for their "signature" weapon class that I feel sad Elden ring doesn't have. Guardian is what every big Havel-cosplayer wishes they could do with their greatshields. Ironeye is the best bows have ever felt to use. Executor is just a return to Sekiro with the ability to parry anything and everything as long as you grind out the timings. Even Scholar, a support character with horrible combat stats, feels so fresh and fun as you slam out buffs and debuffs while juicing your items to massive power levels. There wasn't a single character I've played that I didn't go "Oh wow, I LIKE this one." Except maybe Undertaker.

I do wish there was more map variety. The base game maps are all just the same map, but with a single "interesting zone" slapped in one of the regions. Sometimes it's just Caelid. Sometimes it's the Night city. Sometimes it's a magma chasm. Sometimes it's a snowy mountain. But all of them just amount to: "On the second day, go here and kill your way to the power up." With everything leading up to that feeling pretty same-y after a lot of runs. They added one new map with the DLC which was great, and I do enjoy it a lot. But after a while, it starts to feel the same way.

Ultimately, Nightreign feels like an experiment. And I really hope they learn from this experiment and iterate on it. A new game with a handful of entirely unique maps, each with their own rotating POIs would immediately elevate this already great game. As well as some more weapons, and maybe some new characters. But I can see myself playing this a whole lot more as-is. This game kind of makes it hard to go back to ER just because of how it feels to play.
Almost 200 hours in and I still enjoy playing this game. Overall lives up to the hype of a souls like rogue like set in the Elden Ring series. Would highly suggest giving it a try!
Banger of a game! Recommend it to souls like player who are loking for a chalenger.
Amazing game. Very rewarding and fun to play with friends. You'll have good times with this one.
very fun game. i never really clicked with the og elden ring, but this feels so much better to me. i would heavily recommend if you're a fan of rougelites or soulslike games

Honestly it's the best game i've ever played overall 
i've conquered memories there on my prime and even after i finished i feel like i need to play like i've got the platinum and yet i jst wanna play it lol

After playing for 9 hours, I have only had 3 tries on the Boss (In Solo i got an ego) amazing gameplay for solo players having to deal with a boss whose fight is supposed to be a 3 VS 1. 

I have lost my hair in this span of time. Reccomended for baldies
a great spin off to the souls series highly recommend if you enjoy fast pace coordinated PVE gameplay

An Elden Ring roguelite was my actual dream game. ER is my favorite game of all time, and i regularly play randomizer runs with random builds, level caps and all bosses to make it feel like a proper randomized run just to try and emulate the feeling. For the record, i stopped playing shortly after getting every achievement.

This game utterly fails at doing that. I would genuinely say that the ER randomizer is a better roguelite than this game. The nightlords are mostly all amazing fights, especially Heolstor, who is also one of my favorite designs in Fromsoft, and playing with friends is fun enough, but i am still incredibly disappointed by the state this game got left in.

-Map seeds not actually being random, but only having 25 (with the DLC) dev-made ones for each boss was the biggest mistake you could ever make while making a roguelite. That on top of the map layout being the exact same every run ? Imagine if Isaac only had 100 runs you could play, and wasn't actually randomly generated. Yeah, even with less than 80 hours in, the runs felt extremely samey and that is the worst thing you can do in this genre.

-The souls-like boss formula doesn't work in this framework either, especially when runs are so easy leading up to the nightlord. Everdarks are especially egregious in this regard, where you won't be challenged for 20 minutes, and then basically instantly die to the new boss while learning very little and having to go through another 20 minute slog for another try. I enjoy spending a lot of tries to beat a difficult boss, but i don't enjoy the longest runbacks ever made.

-DLC basically didn't fix any of the issues. Very little new weapons, every new night boss/nightlord is crammed into only two expeditions, the map is once again the exact same every time. I was holding out hope for them to remedy the problems, but they just left it as is, so yeah i skipped the DLC.

-It's been 5 months since the last update, so it's safe to assume this game has been abandoned.

This game is the definition of a 5/10 Potential Man. And it's SO disappointing when the concept was everything i wanted from a video game.
Is gud, anti cheat crap tho game often requires reboot or crash loops
i hope they update the game further but if not its still a cool game as is

dark souls and elden ring, both games teach you the wholesomeness of random coop and invasion, however this game, nightreign, it teaches you another aspect of multiplayer game, it teaches you on how to hate random players and their stupid ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ass !@%$@# 12#! #@$@#$.

still 10/10 game
As far as multiplayer Fromsoft goes, it's alright. Faster than most FS games, still plays okay solo, good stuff. Hard to exactly describe it better beyond it

Very fun game, although if you have an Nvidia graphics card be prepared for massive minor stutters, mind you I have a 4090 and i still get minor stutters and it seems to be only an Nvidia problem but other than that the game is amazing solo or with people
random matchmaking is terrible, great game if you queue with a full stack of friends. awful game if random queuing.
Its basically fast paced, team-based, battle royale Elden Ring and I'm all for it. 10/10 (DLC Map sucks though)

a great game by from software as usual, and seeing as this is their first try at the roguelike genre, i'd say they did a good job.
If you think this is elden ring or dark souls, you are mistaken. Nightreign is a roguelike best enjoyed with a friend or two, can be rather frustrating at times but fun. The nightlords are the peak of boss design imo and their OSTs are just as good.
My only gripe with it is the enemy scaling in duos, their health and damage isn't insane but just above the limit of what could be considered perfect for duos, that they feel too tanky and deal too much dmg. The solo scaling and trio's scaling is perfect, duos just need to be toned down a small bit to be considered perfect
overall a solid 9/10, would reign again with my friend in the night.

All I can say about this game is that it is a whole another experience from your typical souls-like. The fights are pure cinema, the music is very good and the characters are well thought out. 9/10, would absolutely recommend.
Too many skill issue cheaters. Imagine having 500+ hours and using cheated relics in a normal game (yes, not even in DoN). Recommend playing solo or with friends though.
After 240 hrs of gameplay, the Night does indeed Reign as I Elden in the ring

As someone who has found the difficulty of souls–like games to be a barrier to entry, I was delighted that this was the game I was finally able to break through with. I was able to experience, at least to some extent, the highs and lows of the genre; the despair at losing to a boss for the 50th time in a row in conjunction with the high of finally defeating that boss. 

I found the co-op element of the game very helpful for dealing with the roller coaster of emotions you experience playing this game. I was lucky enough to have two friends with whom I could regularly play, thus avoiding the many co-op issues that I know other players have reported. Being able to coordinate and lean on other players helped to overcome some of the more challenging aspects of the game. I would definitely recommend this as a starting place for anybody who liked myself struggles with games in this genre, but wants to eventually become proficient enough in them to play. 

I don’t think this counts quite as a souls-like game; I think it should probably be classified as souls-like-lite. You can sprint, jump, and the overall pacing is rather phrenic since everything is time sensitive. That being said, the timing of a single run is perfect, around 40 minutes to an hour or so, which is perfect for someone like myself who works a full-time job and can’t spend hours regularly playing each day. 

There’s not much customization for focused builds as you might have in other games of the genre. Nonetheless, I found the template builds to be varied, and I enjoyed playing many of them. I think Duchess was probably my favorite, but there were about four that I regularly rotated. I also found that different template builds worked better for different bosses, and it was fun playing around with different combinations for myself and my two companions when trying to defeat different bosses on night 3. 

The game is not perfect. As mentioned above, if you are looking to create a customized build or like tweaking with a specific play style, you will find this game very frustrating. Secondly, this game is significantly better when done with two other players that you know. The lack of any kind of voice chat or other communication except vague pointers makes it much more difficult to play with strangers, and, in my opinion, the game is not particularly fun when playing solo.

Lastly, and this is somewhat of a nitpick, I really dislike the soundtrack. For a game with such cool designs and sweeping landscapes, the music at times actively took me out of what I was trying to enjoy. It felt very simplistic and repetitive. Furthermore, the special events that would alter the maps were too rare. As you  can see from my playtime, I have dedicated a lot of time to the game. But in all those runs, I only came across those special events a couple of times. I would’ve liked to really explore them more thoroughly.

Overall, I really enjoyed the game, especially for as a co-op experience. A solid 7.5 out of 10!

Elden Ring as a Rouge-like, at first i was skeptical but then i remembered that i like these kind of games. Especially the difficulty of this game. Its very fast paced and the Deep of Night mode is very difficult. I like difficulty, although the game does have some small issues here and there, for me its still a good way to blow some steam off. Can only recommend this game to people who played Elden Ring before at the very least and who like a good challenge.
Awesome game basically Elden ring but with other people and more fast paced. Matchmaking is nice you don't have to go look for people to play it just loads up.
Great game if you have friends, which is unfortunate for soulsborne players

The game delivers a fantastic and highly addictive co-op action experience, where teamwork and build coordination make every boss battle incredibly rewarding.

However, since the game is entirely built around multiplayer cooperation , it sorely lacks an in-game voice chat system. 
Having a built-in voice option "especially when matchmaking with random players" would have elevated the experience to a whole new level.

It's hard to overstate the satisfying gameplay loop of Nightreign. Best with friends who know what they're doing. The DLC is your ticket to getting great relics. Embrace the suck, emerge more skilled.

With them adding solos and duos, this is a really fun game to pick up. Sure it's challenging, in a similar way to any Souls-Like, but each of the nightlords are so well designed and fun, and the main loop changes enough from run to run to make it unique each run.

If you enjoy Elden Ring, and have a few friends (or even just one) willing to give it a go with you, it's 100% worth a try.

normal multiplayer without modifications like seamless coop have ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ lag problems due to anticheat/netcode/whatever. its been the same thing for elden ring and ac6. so the normal matchmaking isnt worth it and its only a decent game if you use another application that doesnt even have access to the base game servers.
Tuff ass game i love it but my friends are burnt out of it before i got it and now i dont got anybody to play with
I can heavily recommend, good game, nice balancing, no bugs (thx fromsoft)

Its like Elden Ring and Dark Souls.. but at a blistering pace. Took a while to get into because of how fast the game flow is but once into it, its pretty great.
I play this on a now potato level laptop (compared to this games system requirements). I wouldnt suggest specs any lower than this to play.
Its good thats its multiplayer but its  not good enough no news about bosses no more weapons nothing new to look
Incredibly fun while also being the hardest Fromsoft game to date if you get into the deep of night levels. Had loads of fun with it, definitely worth $40
endlessly fun and rewarding to play. more fun with friends than strangers. ironeye ftw

Great game, I have around 500 hours in the original Elden Ring and this is a great spin off of the OG. I love the fast paced nature of the game. I only wish there was more variety but overall great game.
I have played this game for hundreds of hours on playstation, and it is equally as amazing on pc

This masterpiece is a tapestry of aesthetic splendor, weaving together a diverse array of characters, breathtaking vistas, and immersive worlds, all rendered with uncompromising quality. It is imbued with legendary attention to detail that commands absolute admiration. I award it a well-deserved 9 out of 10, with complete peace of mind and unwavering conviction. It stands, unequivocally, as one of the most sublime gaming experiences one could ever traverse
Roguelike elements are quite fun for a while, although Deep of Night is not well designed at all, the relic farming system is also pretty ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, as well as some of the bosses
Elden Ring but it's a fast-paced roguelite experience. Great gameplay and movement mechanics, another FromSoftware banger.

Have you played Elden Ring? If you have, this is just a much faster paced version of it that includes the Fortnite storm. Depending on who you are, this game could very well be worth the price and you could sink hours into it or alternatively, you could get bored after trying out every character and doing every expedition at least once and never touch it again. I know people of both sides of this aforementioned spectrum and I am the former. I love this game and the gameplay is great and the variety is the only thing I'd say it lacks, but even then, it isn't the worst. The worst is the lack of content given over the game's lifespan. As I am writing this, the game has been out for a year and we have gotten: New relics, harder versions of base bosses, a ranked mode, ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ duos, and a pretty lackluster dlc for $15. This game is absolutely phenomenal and it gets even better if you are willing to mod it and play with a few of your homies. With mods, 9/10. Without, 7/10.

Have you ever had fun running into a brick wall over and over again with friends? Well, I haven't but this game is what I like to think of being that experience! WOULD RECOMMEND IF YOU ENJOYED ELDEN RING!
You wanna do another run of Elden Ring but cannot afford to spend 60-100 hours again? Nightreign is your quick fix! Amazing game!
Game with a lot of potential to be one the best ever made, but it still absolute peak and easily the best roguelike game ever made goated asf
this game is abscolute peak when it comes to bosses no words legit pure cinema i can,t belive a game can pull this much is coop game can,t imagine what more fromsoft can do
Challenging bosses, astonishing visuals, breathtaking music - would recomend!

It's pretty much a faster paced Elden ring experience that focuses more on the combat than exploration. 

Things to note before playing:
1) The runback to the boss is ~40 minutes but each runback will be unique.

2) There's a lot of reading involved as weapons have passives that can stack. Pick up the ones that benefit and/or share it with your teammates

3) The game has poor communication as it only has a pinging system. You can ping weapons/items for teammates, your specific location, or a spot on the map.

Likes:
+ Surge Sprint
+ Teammates can attack the enemy during a riposte/backstab animation
+ Map inspection at any time
+ Unique passives, abilities and ultimates for each class (Nightfarer)
+ Teamplay
+ Weapon passives
+ Bosses from Dark souls Trilogy

Dislikes:
- Relic System 
- Poor Team communication
- Map generation is fixed. (40 map patterns per Nightlord)
- Not enough randomness in Deep of Night
- Inability to pause a run

The relic system is completely RNG and it defines what build you're going for. So someone could have thousands of hours and not get the relic they want. 
The team communication could've been better; request an item or have call outs such as "retreat" or "follow me".
The map isn't random. It will choose between 40 map patterns and if you do remember them, you'll know where the storm will close, which bosses spawn where, etc.
Deep of night can have a random Nightlord or hidden map in Depth 3+. It'd be nice to have more randomness/roguelike conditions to it.
Once an expedition starts, there's no pausing. An option to pause would be great ONLY for a full squad/friends. Solo queue pausing would be unusual.

Play Wylder, go up close to the Nightlord boss, use your ult, stagger the boss, perform a critical hit, die.
Mission accomplished.

Shocked I haven't reviewed after nearly 150 hours, so I'm correcting that. Probably one of the best games I've ever played, I love getting in a game and it just being go mode, nightreign demands attention in a way that I adore every second is valuable from start to finish. Once you've mastered the map theres variations you can play with and every team you wind up playing with gives you a new experience to enjoy, the bosses are all top notch. This game is just a wonderful experiment and why I keep coming back to fromsofts games is because I keep seeing beautiful experiments like this that I get to fall in love with, not to mention the player characters. 

Well what about the story though? Nightreign does it like no other, every story is pieced together brick by brick by playing each individual character and only then do you get to cohesively see the whole story. And truly playing every character is the best way to play the whole game, getting to learn each little intracacy of every character is on of the most rewarding experiences in gaming, every time you do something new with a character it feels like you're uncovering a new layer to them. And these layers beautifully reflect each of their naratives, I love every single one of these characters and much like the rest of the game the only thing I want is more of them. But sadly I won't get that and to see a game I havve well over 100 hours in making me feel that way is truly impressive.

Conclusion one of the best game to ever do it, it's a blast from the word go.

fun game, play with friends, because unless you do, you are going to be paired up with complete randoms, Deep of Night is a blast, but D1-D2 are an absolute slog, they are entirely populated by lobotomites, and D3 has just enough residual limbless eyeless earless stumps from getting carried from D2 to be more of a hindering problem than the nightlords themselves, trudge through until D4-D5 the randoms get better, until then play with your friends or play solo.

It's great to be able to experience the Elden Ring universe through Nightreign. Im enjoying it a lot even though I dont really like being on a rush all the time.

If you're thinking about buying the game, you should give it a go, but you're surely gonna have a rough start even if you watch guides to try to get a good level of general knowlegde. But in a few rounds you'll soon know where to go just by taking a glance at the map.
You're probably also gonna need to google things a lot to get the info you need to play (as usual...).

Having already played Elden Ring and it's DLC, I feel like my biggest, personal issue, with Nightreign is the nature of Souls combat vs. the limited time/encroaching rain. 
From what I've seen so far, in Souls games combat is about knowing enemy patterns and respecting them, and exploiting damage windows. Naturally, dodging and waiting for these damage windows take time... time that often passes all too quickly, specially if you're a new/average player trying to get into the game or master it. 

Anyways, that's just how I feel about it, but these struggles won't stop me from enjoying the game and getting better at it ^_^

Edit: changed to negative cause the game started to stutter and run poorly. I've been investigating in forums, and it turns out this issue is +1year long and there's lots of players who share it, and who have tried many ways to fix it often without success. 
I've played ER and even games like Black Myth Wukong on this PC so it doesnt make sense that Nightreign wouldnt run well, and if they havent bothered to fix this issue after so much time, then it doesnt deserve a positive review.
amazing game. nice bosses great mechanics, nice map
This game is absolute peak with friends, very addictive loop. Made me want to go back to sould-like. Hope they continue releasing updates and DLCs.

You like slamming your head into a wall and never break through it?
Only after an uncertain amount of time depending on how thick your skull is? 
Then this is the game for you! (Actually in a good way)

I was very excited for this game release when it had gotten announced. 

I'm happy to say that I was not disappointed at all. It's exactly what I thought it was going to be and I had a lot of fun. The combat was familiar but also fresh with the tie-in abilities. The reoccurring bosses from previous games was amazing. The nightlords felt extremely rewarding to beat. Though the looting won't always be in your favor; Learning how to combat with what's currently in your hand is a challenging experience that will definitely help you learn and adapt.

The only real challenge that this game has is finding randoms that will stay until the very end. There were countless matches where someone would leave (Clearly due to giving up) and it would just cause the run to go completely downhill. I don't have friends on PC who are into Souls' games. They're all on console. So the lack of crossplay really hinders the experience because I much rather play until I lose rather than just leave and constantly need to feel like I can have a better/easier run. Its a FromSoft game. Nothing is supposed to be easy.

Overall, I really do love this game but without having a real party to play with, the experience can be frustrating since there is no real communication besides pinging. Maybe one day I'll find online friends cool enough to play with.

I have yet to play/own the DLC because I know I would much rather have a party for it. If anyone wants to party up, you can always add me!

Score: 8/10

Great game to wind down after work, especially the getting one shot by the mobs c:

The decision to not have a third day is so perplexing to me and makes it feel like they've fundamentally misunderstood how the expeditions should progress. Because what that decision means is however you prep for the second boss functionally determines your strength during the final boss fight (plus the minimal runes gained from beating the second boss and the shop+grace after). So the player gets the chance to adequately meet the rise in difficulty between the first and second boss, and then... the player's power stagnates right before the hardest part of the run. I've played through Elden Ring and its DLC and while that game *is* hard, it's also fair for the most part. Nightreign doesn't feel fair, because it isn't; It throws two bosses at you one after the other without giving the player a chance to adapt. Yes, adding a third day would make runs take longer and require a lot of tweaking. But it's expected that the game would have to be rebalanced if such a glaring and fundamentally egregious flaw in its progression structure were fixed.

I actually like the game besides this. Sure, having a practice arena would make it way less frustrating rather than every expedition functioning as the longest boss runback in fromsoft history, but overall it scratches a similar itch to Elden Ring. Though, I wholeheartedly believe that fixing those smaller issues won't matter if the progression stays the same. And until that happens (which it most likely won't), Nightreign ultimately feels like a waste of money to me.

I played Elden Ring when it first came out, and I've enjoyed it a bunch. So now that I got Nightreign and played it I have also very much enjoyed it. It's just Elden Ring with friends now pretty much.

If you like to speed run Elden Ring then you probably like this game. Basically this game is a battle royal PVE dark soul game. You have 3 days, the first 2 days you have to explore Limveld, slain bosses at the camp and you will getting runes, armament and buff(these two are RNG) and then around 7 minute the wall of rain will starting and slowly closing in marking the current time from Moring to Noon and Nightfall, at the nightfall you have to face the Night bosses and after defeating them the day will reset, After Night 2 boss is slain the third and final day you will dealing with the Nightlords. If you have not played Elden Ring (or even worst this is your first souls game) i highly do not recommend this game to you because of the game faster pace

This game was legendary after release. The pace of the game, the challenging enemies, the team comps, and the varied playstyles made this game so much fun. I just wish that they supported the game more with content after release. When the game got stale, there wasn't a lot to look forward to, especially for more casual players.

It's really good. Personally I'm a huge fan of the Executor class because the playstyle is similar to Sekiro deflection but in the roguelike setting it provides novel experiences every play session. Good with 2 players, great with 3.
